feed.title: Google Cloud: Knowledge Catalog
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Knowledge Catalog

entry.content:
Knowledge Catalog connectors for importing metadata from Oracle and MySQL data sources are available in preview.

Knowledge Catalog connectors automatically extract metadata (technical, operational, and business) from external data sources and import it into Knowledge Catalog entry groups. You can schedule metadata import runs on a set schedule.

For more information, see About database connectors and Manage connector configurations.
entry.summary: Knowledge Catalog connectors for Oracle and MySQL data sources are now available in preview. These connectors automatically extract and import technical, operational, and business metadata into Knowledge Catalog, with options for scheduled import runs. This feature simplifies metadata management for your data sources.
entry.title: Feature: Metadata connectors for Oracle and MySQL now in preview

entry.content:
Knowledge Catalog now supports data profile scans for unstructured data (such as PDFs in Cloud Storage) on existing BigQuery object tables. This feature uses Vertex AI Gemini models to extract semantic insights, including entities and relationships, from unstructured content.

Note: Data profile scans for unstructured data are currently available in Preview using the Dataplex REST API only. The cloud console and gcloud workflows are not supported for this feature.

For more information, see About unstructured data insights and Use data profile for unstructured data.
entry.summary: Knowledge Catalog now offers data profile scans for unstructured data, including PDFs in Cloud Storage, on BigQuery object tables. This new feature leverages Vertex AI Gemini models to extract valuable semantic insights from your unstructured content. Currently in preview, it's accessible via the Dataplex REST API.
entry.title: Feature: Unstructured Data Insights Available

entry.content:
Starting June 1, 2026, the Data Catalog service begins a phased shutdown. From this date onward, you might experience disruptions or a complete lack of access to Data Catalog APIs. Knowledge Catalog (formerly known as Dataplex Catalog) operates without impact.

For more information about migrating from Data Catalog to Knowledge Catalog, see Transition from Data Catalog to Knowledge Catalog.
entry.summary: The Data Catalog service will be shut down starting June 1, 2026, potentially causing disruptions. Knowledge Catalog remains unaffected. Please refer to the documentation for migration details.
entry.title: Deprecated: Data Catalog Deprecation

entry.content:
You can use the lookupContext method to retrieve a pre-formatted bundle of data asset context optimized for interactive agentic workflows. This LLM-ready context helps to ground your agents in assessing and using data assets.

This feature is available in preview.

For more information, see Retrieve context for data assets.
entry.summary: You can now use the lookupContext method to retrieve a pre-formatted bundle of data asset context. This LLM-ready context helps to ground your agents in assessing and using data assets. This feature is currently in preview.
entry.title: Feature: New way to get data asset context

entry.content:
The Data Lineage API includes the searchLineageStreaming method that performs a breadth-first search (upstream or downstream) to retrieve lineage links for an asset identified by its Fully Qualified Name (FQN).

For more information, see the Data Lineage API reference for REST.
entry.summary: We've added a new method, searchLineageStreaming, to the Data Lineage API. This method allows you to perform upstream or downstream searches to retrieve lineage links for an asset using its Fully Qualified Name (FQN).
entry.title: Feature: New API method for data lineage search

entry.content:
You can use the data lineage remote MCP server to interact with Knowledge Catalog (formerly Dataplex Universal Catalog) to query data lineage graphs, discover upstream data provenance, and analyze downstream impact.

This feature is available in preview. For more information, see Use the data lineage remote MCP server.
entry.summary: Use the new data lineage remote MCP server to query lineage graphs and discover data provenance within Knowledge Catalog. This feature helps you understand upstream sources and downstream impacts of your data. It is currently available in preview.
entry.title: Feature: Interact with Knowledge Catalog for Data Lineage

entry.content:
Data products in Knowledge Catalog is Generally Available (GA). A data product serves as a logical, curated package of data assets and context designed to solve a specific business problem.

This release includes the following new features:

  • Approval workflows for data product consumption: Data product consumers can browse published data products, submit access requests, and track their status. Data product owners can track, approve, or reject access requests using the Google Cloud Console or the API. For more information, see Use data products and Manage data products.
  • Automated documentation and insights: Data product owners can leverage Knowledge Catalog data insights and Gemini to automatically generate sample queries, business insights, and documentation templates for data products. For more information, see Create data products.
  • Service account support: Data product owners can configure service accounts in access groups, and data product consumers can request access for their service accounts. For more information, see Create data products.
  • Remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server support (Preview) Data applications and AI agents can programmatically interact with data products. By deploying the Knowledge Catalog remote MCP server, developers can create data products, discover data products, and inspect data product metadata from external IDEs and LLM clients. For more information, see Access data products using Model Context Protocol.
entry.summary: Knowledge Catalog's data products are now Generally Available, offering curated packages of data assets to solve business problems. This release introduces approval workflows for data consumption, automated documentation generation with Gemini, service account support, and preview support for remote Model Context Protocol server interactions.
entry.title: Feature: Data Products are Now Generally Available

entry.content: Column-level lineage for Dataproc is generally available (GA). This feature enables you to track the flow of data between individual columns in BigQuery, BigLake external tables, Cloud Storage buckets, and other resources as reported by Dataproc clusters and Serverless for Apache Spark. For more information, see About data lineage.
entry.summary: Track data flow between columns in BigQuery, BigLake, Cloud Storage, and more, as reported by Dataproc. This new feature provides granular visibility into your data's journey across various resources. Understand how data transforms and moves at the column level for better data governance and analysis.
entry.title: Feature: Column-level lineage for Dataproc now available

entry.content:
The Data Lineage API is now updated with the following changes:

  • The SearchLinks method accepts multiple source and target entity references as search criteria.
  • Added support for column-level lineage information to be passed and returned from the service.
  • Process resources now report Dataflow as their origin if it is used to generate lineage.

For more information, see the Data Lineage API reference for REST and RPC.
entry.summary: The Data Lineage API has been updated to support multiple entity references in searches and to include column-level lineage information. Additionally, Process resources now correctly identify Dataflow as their origin when it's used for lineage generation.
entry.title: Feature: Data Lineage API Enhancements

entry.content: Cloud Composer is now called Managed Service for Apache Airflow. The names for associated APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) remain unchanged and still reference Composer.
entry.summary: Cloud Composer has been renamed to Managed Service for Apache Airflow. While the service name has changed, all associated APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and IAM references will continue to use the name 'Composer'.
entry.title: Announcement: Managed Service for Apache Airflow is here

entry.content: Dataproc and Google Cloud Serverless for Apache Spark are now unified under the Managed Service for Apache Spark brand. This change consolidates our managed Spark deployment options into a single umbrella brand that includes the full breadth of our Spark capabilities. No existing functionality is being removed as part of this change, and there is no impact to the Dataproc API, metastore, client library, CLI, or IAM names.
entry.summary: Dataproc and Google Cloud Serverless for Apache Spark are now unified under the Managed Service for Apache Spark brand. This consolidation offers a single umbrella for all Spark capabilities without removing any existing functionality or impacting current APIs.
entry.title: Announcement: Unified Spark Service on Google Cloud

entry.content: BigLake is now called Google Cloud Lakehouse. BigLake metastore is now called the Lakehouse runtime catalog. The names for associated APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) remain unchanged and still reference BigLake.
entry.summary: BigLake has been renamed to Google Cloud Lakehouse, and its metastore is now the Lakehouse runtime catalog. Existing APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and IAM references still use the BigLake name.
entry.title: Announcement: BigLake renamed to Google Cloud Lakehouse

entry.content: Knowledge Catalog discovers links between data assets, helping you understand how they connect and the nature of their relationships. This feature is available in preview. For more information, see View data relationships in Knowledge Catalog.
entry.summary: Knowledge Catalog now helps you discover links between your data assets, revealing how they connect and the nature of their relationships. This feature is currently available in preview. Learn more about viewing data relationships in Knowledge Catalog.
entry.title: Feature: Discover data relationships

entry.content: Data quality now supports rule reusability. You can now define data quality rules as templates and reuse them across multiple catalog entries to standardize your data quality processes. You can also use a shared library of system rule templates for common data validation scenarios. For more information, see Reuse data quality rules.
entry.summary: Enhance your data quality processes with reusable rule templates. Define custom templates or leverage a shared library of system templates for consistent data validation across your catalog entries.
entry.title: Feature: Data Quality Rule Reusability

entry.content:
You can now build and run a Knowledge Catalog discovery agent to get more relevant search results for complex natural language queries.

For more information, see Build an agent to discover your data.
entry.summary: Build and run a Knowledge Catalog discovery agent to improve search result relevance for natural language queries. This new feature helps you find the data you need more effectively.
entry.title: Feature: Enhance search with discovery agents

entry.content: To further refine lineage graphs, Knowledge Catalog lineage views include new highlight and filter modes. This feature is available in preview. For more information, see Apply filters and highlighting for a focused view.
entry.summary: Knowledge Catalog lineage views now offer highlight and filter modes to help you refine lineage graphs. This feature is available in preview and allows for a more focused view of your data lineage.
entry.title: Feature: New lineage graph views

entry.content:
Data insights for unstructured data transforms dark data or unstructured files in the form of PDFs in Cloud Storage into structured, queryable assets. This feature is now available in preview.

For more information, see About data insights for unstructured data.
entry.summary: Turn your unstructured files like PDFs in Cloud Storage into structured, queryable assets with the new data insights feature. This powerful tool helps you unlock valuable information from your dark data. It is now available in preview, allowing you to explore its capabilities.
entry.title: Feature: Unstructured Data Insights Now in Preview

entry.content:
Automated cataloging of Iceberg REST Catalog (IRC) for Google Cloud Lakehouse runtime catalog is now generally available (GA). This includes support for lineage, data profiling, data quality, and data insights.

Federated support for Databricks Unity IRC, AWS Glue Data Catalog IRC, and Snowflake Horizon IRC is available in preview.

For more information, see About metadata management in Knowledge Catalog.
entry.summary: The Lakehouse runtime catalog is now generally available, offering automated cataloging for Iceberg REST Catalog. This release includes enhanced support for lineage, data profiling, data quality, and data insights, along with preview support for federated catalogs from Databricks, AWS Glue, and Snowflake.
entry.title: Feature: New Cataloging Features for Lakehouse Runtime

entry.content: Dataplex Universal Catalog is now called Knowledge Catalog. The API, client library, CLI, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) names remain unchanged.
entry.summary: Dataplex Universal Catalog has been renamed to Knowledge Catalog. While the name has changed, the API, client library, CLI, and IAM names will remain the same.
entry.title: Announcement: Dataplex Universal Catalog is now Knowledge Catalog

entry.content:
The lightweight profiling mode for data profile scans is available in preview.

The lightweight mode provides low-latency profile scans that return results in seconds, making it ideal for grounding AI agent responses and interactive data exploration. For more information, see Profiling modes.
entry.summary: A new lightweight profiling mode for data profile scans is now available in preview. This mode offers low-latency scans, returning results in seconds, which is perfect for AI grounding and interactive data exploration.
entry.title: Feature: Lightweight Data Profiling Mode Now in Preview

entry.content:
You can now specify a custom execution identity for data quality and data profile scans. By default, scans are executed using the Service Agent. You can now use a custom service account (Bring Your Own Service Account) or End-User Credentials (EUC). Using a custom execution identity lets you enforce the principle of least privilege, use fine-grained BigQuery access controls, and unify scan processing costs directly under BigQuery.

For more information, see Configure execution identity for data quality scans and Configure execution identity for data profile scans.
entry.summary: You can now specify a custom service account or end-user credentials for data quality and data profile scans, enhancing security and cost management. This allows for least privilege access and unified cost tracking within BigQuery.
entry.title: Feature: Custom execution identity for scans
